objective: Close the residual governance-source enforcement gaps left after issue #583 so the waterfall is hard-gated at source before downstream rollout resumes.
scope_boundary: Issue-backed plan, scope, handoff, review, validation, and closeout artifacts for the residual source-level enforcement fixes.
Validator, schema, local-CI profile, workflow, standards, and runbook changes needed to close the residual gaps documented in issue #585.
Replay proof against existing downstream failure shapes without mutating downstream repos in this lane.
out_of_scope: Direct product-repo repair in Stampede or other governed consumers in this branch.
Reopening the already-merged issue-583 source fixes except where required by the residual findings.
Warning-only or documentation-only treatment of the residual enforcement gaps.
Unrelated bootstrap, graphify, or provider wiring changes.
research_summary: Post-merge audit of issue #583 confirmed the merged source blocks the originally reproduced bad packet and stale consumer-record shapes, but specialist review found four residual governance-source gaps: feat/issue branch shapes can still bypass mandatory packet creation if no plan exists, alternate_model_review is not yet machine-checked as genuinely alternate-family, handoff/review refs are checked only for shape rather than existence, and session/runbook enforcement still validates mostly presence while runbook-only changes can bypass the local session gate.

sprints.name: Packet and review
sprints.goal: Capture the residual source-level gap set, bounded acceptance criteria, and alternate-family review before implementation-ready promotion.
tasks: Write the issue-585 PDCAR, structured plan, planning execution scope, and planned handoff.
Capture specialist review on whether the residual gaps are correctly bounded and still blocking for downstream rollout.
Run the governed Claude review path against the packet before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: The issue-585 packet validates locally as planning_only.
The packet names the exact residual source gaps and blocks downstream rollout until they are closed.
Alternate-family review is recorded through the governed wrapper path before implementation-ready promotion.

sprints.name: Residual source hardening
sprints.goal: Land the minimum governance-source fixes that close the residual enforcement gaps and prove them locally.
tasks: Patch the structured-plan schema, validator, and tests for broader issue-branch coverage, alternate-family identity enforcement, and referenced-artifact existence checks.

Patch the session-contract validator and local-CI session gate scope so runbook-only changes cannot bypass semantic session-contract enforcement, including the canonical bootstrap command, `--emit-hook-note` helper contract, and wrapper-to-helper path.
Update standards and runbook text only as needed to keep the source contract aligned with the new machine checks.
acceptance_criteria: Any branch whose name resolves through `_branch_issue_number()` fails the mandatory-plan gate when no matching structured plan exists, including feat/issue branch shapes.
Same-family or same-identity alternate review claims fail deterministically, with accepted alternate review carrying `reviewer_model_id` and `reviewer_model_family` fields for machine comparison.
Missing referenced handoff/review artifacts fail deterministically when the evidence gate applies.
Runbook-only or session bootstrap helper changes trigger the local session-contract gate.
The strengthened source checks are covered by regression tests and local proof.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm the residual issue-585 source gaps are still blocking for downstream rollout and are correctly bounded.
specialist_reviews.summary: The residual gaps are real and source-owned: branch-shape packet enforcement, alternate-family proof, referenced-artifact existence checks, and session/runbook semantic gating still need hardening before downstream rollout resumes.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: Claude specialist review via scripts/codex-review.sh claude
alternate_model_review.summary: Accepted with follow-up: implementation-ready promotion may proceed after recording clean revalidation proof and carrying the concrete schema/gate details into the implementation lane.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #585 is approved for the bounded governance-source hardening work: issue-token branch gating, alternate-review identity proof, artifact existence checks, and session/runbook semantic gate coverage.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Apply the source-level validator, schema, local-CI, standards, and runbook fixes inside the implementation scope and verify them with regression proof.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: The issue-585 implementation remains inside the bounded source-level validator, schema, session-contract, local-CI, bootstrap-helper, runbook, and proof surfaces named in the implementation scope.
Downstream rollout remains blocked until this residual governance-source lane is closed.

material_deviation_rules: Do not resume downstream rollout on the strength of issue-583 alone.
Do not weaken residual findings into documentation-only warnings.
Do not widen this governance-source lane into direct consumer-repo implementation work.
